pattern: Fixing the left end and sweeping the right end while keeping a running sum and a running set of the members seen so far turns a quadratic-looking membership question into cheap constant-time checks per subarray.
hints: Every single-element subarray is automatically centered, since its sum is always equal to its only element.
For a fixed left endpoint, grow the right endpoint one step at a time, keeping a running total of the subarray sum and a running collection of which values are currently inside it.
At each right endpoint, the subarray is centered exactly when the running total is one of the values currently in that running collection; sum this indicator over every left and right endpoint pair.

edge_inputs.description: the reference small case mixing negative, positive and zero
two elements, only the singletons are centered
all zeros, every subarray sum is zero and zero is always present
single element at the minimum allowed value
single element at the maximum allowed value
all identical positive values, sums grow past any single member quickly
increasing run where the total sum never matches a member after the first couple of elements
negatives that cancel to reproduce an early value later in the array

stress.description: random values across the full range at a moderate size
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 500))
    nums = [rng.randint(-100000, 100000) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]

stress.description: small-magnitude values at the maximum length, so sums frequently coincide with members
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 500))
    nums = [rng.randint(-5, 5) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]
